heading: Rural development
label: 2204b–1 Rural development
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (b), was repealed and a new section 1926(a)(7) was added by , , . As added, defined “rural areas” but no longer contained provisions relating to a private business enterprise exception. Subsequently, section 1926(a)(7) was repealed by , , .Section 1926(a)(7) of this titlePub. L. 104–127, title VII, § 741(a)(2)110 Stat. 1123section 1926(a)(7) of this titlePub. children.heading: Congressional commitment
children.label: a Congressional commitment
children.text: The Congress commits itself to a sound balance between rural and urban America. The Congress considers this balance so essential to the peace, prosperity, and welfare of all our citizens that the highest priority must be given to the revitalization and development of rural areas.

children.heading: Location of Federal facilities
children.label: b Location of Federal facilities
children.text: Congress hereby directs the heads of all executive departments and agencies of the Government to establish and maintain departmental policies and procedures giving first priority to the location of new offices and other facilities in rural areas as defined in the private business enterprise exception in section 1926(a)(7) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1
